title: aspalathus juniperina subsp. gracilifolia
intro: Aspalathus juniperina subsp. gracilifolia, also known as rooibos or red bush, is a shrubby plant species native to South Africa, belonging to the legume family. Traditionally, its leaves have been used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits associated with consuming rooibos tea include reducing the risk of certain cancers, improving heart health, and alleviating symptoms of allergies and asthma. Rooibos tea can be prepared by infusing the leaves in hot water, or it can be found in various herbal preparations such as capsules, tinctures, and granules.
